entry.title: Overview of all GenAI Options in SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Introduction 

SAP Build Code, the pro-code offering utilizing Joule,  brings cutting-edge generative AI (GenAI) capabilities to developers across a wide array of use cases. These range from UI5 and Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) development to providing assistance on Mobile Development Kit (MDK) projects or HANA development.

Since its general availability in March 2024, which initially highlighted AI features primarily for CAP, Joule's GenAI capabilities have expanded significantly. As new features continue to be introduced, this blog post aims to summarize the current features and the variety of use cases they support. Furthermore, as the approach for the use of different functionalities may differ, we will also provide some recommendations that are specific to some features.

Before introducing the list we will dive shortly into what prompting is as well as talk about the difference between general LLM use and Joule for develompent.

**Note: This blog will be updated from time to time to reflect upcoming and new features 

What are Prompts? 

"Prompt" is a verb that means to cause, help, or instruct someone or something to do something. In information technology, "prompt" can refer to the "command prompt". 

Prompts are used in GenAI, including SAP Joule, across several topics. A prompt can be a command, input, or a piece of text provided to initiate a response or action. SAP Joule can utilize prompts as instructions to propose code. 

An example of a simple prompt is: "Create an incident management app." 

 

Importance of well-crafted Prompts in generating Code 

Generative AI models – including the ones used by SAP Joule – use prompts as a point of initiation, where they are given initial instructions or context to generate content. Artificial intelligence capabilities are especially useful for tasks that don’t need to consider things like cultural differences. So, app development is a great candidate for being supported using prompts. Effective prompt engineering implies creating well-structured, directive prompts that steer AI responses to ensure they are accurate and relevant to the context. 

The code generated by SAP Joule significantly depends on the quality of input prompts. Therefore, the precision and relevance of the output can be greatly improved by constructing high-quality prompts that draw out the intended response. Such carefully engineered prompts can notably boost user experience and task coherence. 

It is important to remember that crafting an effective prompt necessitates creativity. It is your responsibility to ensure your expected output is clearly conveyed to the model. This commonly includes comprehension of how the model perceives different prompts and the appropriate rephrasing of them.

 

Differences between general LLM use and using Joule for coding tasks inside  SAP Business Application Studio 

Prompt engineering is essential when using a general LLM to ensure good response quality. Prompt engineering methods such as Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting have been shown to achieve better results. Chain-of-Thought prompting involves guiding individuals to articulate their reasoning step-by-step, helping them clarify their thoughts and explore the reasoning behind their conclusions. This technique encourages deeper cognitive engagement and can enhance problem-solving and decision-making skills by making the thought process explicit. An example of this could be adding the following to the prompt:

“[prompt question]. Please break down your thought process step-by-step.” 

Joule in SAP Build Code, however, is specialized in supporting pro-code developers in coding tasks. It provides predefined slash commands for a variety of use cases, such as UI5, Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), Mobile, and HANA development. Due to the specialization of Joule, the prompt will already be matched and improved, thus reducing the manual effort to make use of extensive prompt engineering methods. 

This chapter will further depict best practices for using Joule in SAP Build Code. 

 

Be clear, specific, and directive  

Generally, in prompt engineering it is always good to provide more context while being clear on what you want. The more precise information the prompt contains, the better Joule will understand what you really want to achieve. Example:  

  • Vague Prompt: "I need a purchase order tracking application."  
  • Improved Prompt: "Develop a purchase order tracking application where entity 'Purchase Order' is associated to entity 'Supplier'. The 'Purchase Order' consists of the following fields: ID, Quantity, Price. Entity 'Supplier' consist of the fields  ID, Name, Contact Person, Mail Address"  

It should be noted that, instead of adding more and more complexity and context into one prompt where Joule for example is used to generate multiple files (e.g. for UI5 development), in some cases breaking complex tasks into simpler ones may be a better approach.  

 

Provide Examples 

Additionally, examples provide meaningful information on how the output should be structured or content should be generated. It can be extremely helpful in some scenarios, for example in sample data generation: 

  • Before: "Create meaningful sample data for the 'Purchase Order' and 'Supplier' entities.” 
  • Improved Prompt: "Create a set of meaningful sample data for the 'Purchase Order' and 'Supplier' entities. The purchase order data should be targeted at German customers and must use the euro currency symbol (€) and the German abbreviation for quantity units (e.g., 'STK' for Stück). Each Purchase Order ID should be exactly 6 digits long. Include at least 20 records. Example format: Purchase Order - ID: 000109; Quantity: 10 STK; Price: 45,25€."

 

Use of context-awareness 

Joule in SAP Build Code in general is context aware of the project you created in the SAP Build Lobby. Depending on the project type (Full-Stack, Fiori, Mobile), certain slash commands will be suggested. Besides that, Joule can make use of files in the project. Depending on the slash command and the prompt description, only required files are considered in the prompt, thereby eliminating the need to use more input data (code) than necessary for the response generation.  

Furthermore, you can make use of the #-sign in a prompt to include files which should be taken into account by Joule. This feature use will be explained in more detail in the following sections.

 

GenAI Options in SAP Build Code 

Joule offers specific skills for SAP app development with SAP Build Code which can be requested using slash commands (“/”). As each slash command serves a different purpose, the required input as well as the output format from within Joule can vary. Therefore, there are best practices for some of the tasks, which will be highlighted in this section. 

 

CAP 

Joule offers skills for the generation and development of CAP. The following slash commands are suggested when creating a Full-Stack Application Project:

/cap-gen-app 

Using this slash command, Joule will create a new CAP application along with some sample data based on the prompt. It is recommended to be clear and precise while providing a good amount of context. The following example highlights how being more specific and providing more context can result in better results. 

  • Example: “ I would like to create an incident management application. Can you do that for me?” 

This may provide already some good looking results. However, as in most cases requirements already exist for the entities, its associations, and fields, being more specific can provide the desired outcomes.

With a more specific prompt: 

  • Example: “Create an app where customers can report incidents. Include a 'Customer' entity with fields Customer ID, Name, Email, and Phone Number. Each 'Incident' should include Incident ID, Description, DateReported (Date), and Status. Each incident also includes replacement items, and each replacement item is linked to a product. The 'ReplacementItem' entity should have fields Replacement ID, Quantity , and ReplacementDate (Date). The 'Product' entity should have fields Product ID, Product Name, Category, and ExpiryDate (Date). Use associations instead of compositions for the relationships between entities.” 

In this example, the named entities are defined with the properties coming from requirements. Providing comprehensive descriptions of entities and associations ensures that Joule closely adheres to the application's context during creation. Since Joule prioritizes the creation of compositions over associations, it is advisable to explicitly specify the use of associations as illustrated in the example above. 

If the generated data from the data model does not meet expectations, there is the option to either regenerate the entire dataset to obtain a potentially more suitable outcome, or preferably, use the /cap-edit-model command to make adjustments. 

 

/cap-edit-model 

By using this command, you can edit an existing data model: 

As displayed in the screenshot, a context variable must be entered with the #-sign, so that Joule knows which data model to modify.  

But it is more than just editing an existing data model. You can do code reviews, add comments and much more. You can easily ask like “Does this code follow the best pratices of CAP?” 

You can find more examples in this blog .

 

/cap-gen-data and /cap-edit-data 

Sample data from the CAP app generation can be generated to match realistic values. For general sample data, a vaguer prompt can be used: 

  • Example: “Create realistic values for the sample data” 

Of course, with more precise information, the data will be matched better to the desired format: 

  • Example: “Create realistic values for the sample data. For the Product Entity, make sure the `criticality` values are in the range of [1(Negative), 2(Critical), 3(Positive)]. [...]” 

For further editing the created sample data or adapting existing sample data of a specific entity, /cap-edit-data slash command should be used. A context variable referencing the csv file with existing sample data has to be provided. 

To ensure the generated data meets expectations, it is crucial to use clear and precise prompts. For instance, indicating specific ranges, formats, or patterns can help tailor the output to match the desired criteria. Do not include any detail about the application logic in the data model as it will be ignored by Joule. 

 

/cap-app-logic 

A further pivotal command in the CAP suite is the /cap-app-logic command, which enables the creation and modification of application logic. This command requires a context variable and an application logic file, serving as prerequisites. For more intricate application logic, breaking down the task into smaller, manageable modifications is advisable. 

 

/cap-unit-test 

To create unit tests, this command should be executed after the application logic has been implemented. Considering the context variable of the application logic file, the provided sample prompt for generating a unit test may already be adequate. If additional test scenarios or modifications are necessary, the slash command can be invoked again to make the required adjustments. For unit tests, provide the appropriate data for positive and negative cases to make sure the tests pass accordingly. 

 

Fiori/UI5 

This section describes the use of slash commands for Fiori development, which can be UI5 freestyle apps, Fiori Elements apps, or a mixture between these two using the flexible programming model. 

/fiori-gen-spec-app 

In addition to /cap-gen-app, the /fiori-gen-spec-app slash command facilitates the rapid creation of SAP Fiori applications by translating business requirements into a CAP project with a SAP Fiori elements application. This command leverages the GenAI capabilities to interpret text or image-based inputs, defining entities, associations, and UI layouts based on your specifications. It's designed to aid developers by generating list reports, object pages, and form entry object pages, all while supporting draft-enabled applications by default. 

Since this creates a whole application, it is suggested to provide enough and precise context of the business requirements to the prompt to ensure accurate generation and thus avoid regenerating the whole project. This is also important as regenerating the project will create a completely new project without being able to take over specific parts. Here, Joule also supports an image/sketch as an input.

  • Example (Text):

 

“ I want to create an SAP Fiori application that shows my customers with their contracts. Each customer has the following attributes: 
1. Name 
2. Identification Number 
3. Address 
4. Contract Information 
5. A contract is referencing a customer. One customer can have multiple 
  contracts. Attributes of a contract include: 
6. Contract ID: A unique identifier for the contract 
7. Customer: Information about the customer involved 
8. Contract Type: The type of contract (sales, service, employment) 
9. Start Date: The date on which the contract becomes valid 
10. End Date: The date on which the contract expires (if applicable) 
11. Status: The current status of the contract (draft, active, expired, terminated, etc.) 

When starting the CAP application I first want to see a list of customers. 
When selecting a specific row in that list, I want to see to a page with details of that customer. 

It should show the following sections: 
- section "Customer Info" displaying the attributes of the selected customer 
- section "Contracts" showing a list of all contracts for this customer 

When selecting a specific row in the contracts list, I want to see a page with details of that contract. This page should show two sections: 

- section "Contract Info" containing: 
 - contract ID 
 - Customer Name 
 - Contract Type 
 - Status 
- section "Validity" containing 
 - Start Date 
 - End Date “

 

  • Example (Image):  

For more information, you can refer to the documentation of this slash command. 

 

/ui5 

The /ui5 slash command offers a broad support for many of the topics resolving around UI5 development. For example, it can be used to create and modify files in a freestyle project. This can range from adding controls and application logic to helping with configuration of the project. Furthermore, if asked, Joule can help understand UI5 related artifacts. Besides freestyle development, it also provides help understand Fiori elements and other UI5 related applications. Being clear and precise is recommended for using this command when generating code. It will try to automatically figure out which files in the project are needed as context and include these. At this stage, however, it does not know anything about e.g. the metadata of a remote service. 

  • Example: “Create a table for the main view based on the entity set ‘Products’ of the OData service, which is represented in the local metadata file. Add the following three columns and bindings to the table: ProductID, Name, Quantity.” 
  • Example: “Add a column to the table in the main view which displays a button called ‘Order’. If the user presses the button, show a message toast with the message ‘Order clicked’” 
  • Example: “What does the code in the main controller do?” 
  • Example: “How can I configure an OData service to be used in the app?” 

Furthermore, the prompt can also consider the currently selected code. For example, if a function inside a controller file is selected, the user can simply ask Joule to explain that code without the need to specify the file in the prompt. 

 

/ui5-typescript 

The SAPUI5 TypeScript assistant can be used for converting a SAPUI5 application project (or single files in it) to TypeScript. It can do both, the creation of the appropriate project setup that handles transpilation, as well as converting JavaScript files to TypeScript, which includes the required structural changes in the files, like using ECMAScript (ES) module imports and ES classes. 

The converted or added files will be listed and can be clicked to inspect a diff view of the changes before accepting them. The diff may be extensive, as such a conversion typically touches many lines. Still, this offers an opportunity to identify any unintended changes. 

A certain amount of re-work of the result may be needed, e.g. to adjust some of the generated return types of functions or to add needed typecasts. 

The functionality itself tries to convert everything inside a project at once. However, in order ut to avoid being hit by size limits and inaccuracies, it is recommended to proceed in smaller steps. For example, first convert only the project setup, but no JavaScript files. Then migrate single or few files at once, depending on their size. It is also recommended to only use this assistant for SAPUI5 application code. 

This assistant is generally available inside SAPUI5 projects. 

  • Example: “Migrate the setup of the project to TypeScript. Do not migrate JS files yet.” 
  • Example: “Migrate the Main controller to TypeScript” 
  • Example: “Migrate the current file to TypeScript” 

Further information can be found in this blog post .

 

/ui5-create-page 

The SAPUI5 Create Page assistant creates a new UI5 view with a corresponding controller and configures routing for seamless navigation to this new page. 

This command scaffolds a new XML view and JavaScript or TypeScript controller, updates the manifest.json to include the necessary routing configurations, and provides default code for controller functionality. It can also add code to existing controllers which trigger the navigation to the new page. It is ideal for quickly setting up additional screens or views in your UI5 project. 

  • Example: “Create a new page with controller and routing setup. The new page should contain an address form, navigation should be triggered by a new button in the header of the Main view.” 

 

Mobile 

For mobile app development using mobile services, Joule also offers slash commands. The prerequisite for using these slash commands is having a mobile app project. 

/mdk-gen-i18n 

This command generates i18n translation files in multiple languages, each containing key-value pairs of strings. Based on your business needs, you can create additional translation files for various languages from a source file (e.g. i18n.properties). This enables you to reach a wide audience and ensure that your application is accessible to users in diverse regions. It is recommended to provide the required languages in the prompt itself: 

  • Example: “Please generate i18n files for the following languages: German, Dutch, Italian, and French.” 
  • Example: “Please generate i18n file in German language.” 

Once accepted, you must choose whether to overwrite or merge the changes. Merge file option append any new changes to existing files. Overwrite file option completely replaces an existing file with the new changes. 

 

/mdk-gen-page  

This command generates a new page containing MDK UI controls. Also, it may also produce other related application definitions, such as Action, other associated Page, Rule or Style files, with the page. The service definition is considered when creating the page. Nonetheless, it is possible to specify the desired layout appearance: 

  • Example: “Please generate a customer list page using object table section where firstname and lastname are displayed as headline. The other labels should be empty. 
  • Example: “Please generate a page displaying customer information in a contact cell control.” 

 

/mdk-gen-action 

This command generates a new MDK action. Also, it may also produce other related application definitions, such as Page or other associated Action files, with the Action. The service definition is considered when creating the Action. 

  • Example: “Please generate an action displaying product information.” 
  • Example: “Please generate an action updating product entity.” 
  • Example: “Please generate an action to call SAP AI chat completions API.” 

Note: All the MDK related slash commands are also accessible for a mobile project created in a full-stack project. 

 

HANA

/hana-gen 

This command enables users to create SAP HANA Cloud projects by specifying requirements in natural language. The tool interprets these requirements and generates a complete project structure, including database artifacts such as tables, views, and sample data. This functionality is particularly useful for rapid prototyping and development, ensuring consistency and reducing manual effort. Example prompts: 

  • Example: “Create an inventory management project and use schema name as 'inventory'.” 
  • Example: “Create a flight management project.” 
  • Example: “Create a bookshop management project and use namespace as 'com.sap' and use schema name as 'bookshop'” 

 

Other Slash Commands 

/help 

The /help command displays a list of prompts available in Joule for SAP Build Code. This includes example prompts and descriptions of each prompt. 

 

/code-search 

Natural language can be utilized to search for code and obtain a concise summary of its functionality. The first time a search is performed, it may take some moments for the results to appear because the workspace content needs to be indexed. The results show the files relevant to the search criteria. 

The next time a code search is performed, Joule suggests prompts based on the previous search. Any new file that has been added to the workspace is indexed for code search. 

 

Inline Code-Completion  

Inline code-completion is a feature that provides code snippet suggestions directly in the editor itself (e.g. for JavaScript) based on your typing input the the context. It will be enabled per default. To use inline code-completion effectively, you can accept a full suggestion by pressing Tab or reject it with Esc. For more control, cmd/ctrl + right arrow (→) can be used to accept suggestions word-by-word. The autocomplete suggestion considers the code around the cursor and recent files to provide a matching suggestion. Furthermore, it may be helpful to write a comment with the desired output to get an autocomplete suggestion: 

  • Example: “// Create a Message Box Popup which has three actions: Save, Draft, and Cancel” 

In the example above, after pressing enter, an autocomplete suggestion will be provided based on the comment.  

 

Final Remarks 

While utilizing GenAI functionality can enhance development efficiency, it is recommended to thoroughly review the proposed code before implementation. Although Joule strives to deliver optimal results, Language Learning Models (LLMs) are not perfect. Additionally, response generation may differ even when using identical prompts. Therefore, if the outcome is unsatisfactory, you may either regenerate the response with the same prompt or revise the prompt to achieve better results.  

For example, if a data schema has been created that mostly meets the requirements but is missing a property or definition, it may be an option to rewrite the prompt by including the provided structure from the last prompt and specifically describing what parts are missing.  

As Joule in SAP Build Code is tailored to specific use cases, a don’t is to ask questions unrelated to the slash commands. An example would be, to ask for the weather, request SAP unspecific text generation, etc. The core strength of Joule within SAP Build Code and the Business Application Studio lies in its powerful tailored capabilities, where a clear and precise prompt will help you with what you want to achieve.

entry.title: SAPUI5: Consume destination during local development]
entry.content:
Fellow friends, i'm having trouble using my sapui5 app with a destination while working locally.
When deployed, the app works perfectly, but when i'm in development mode on vscode i have a cors error and can't get to access the data.
Basically, it is a simple destination pointing to a NorthWind oData api, no credentials are needed. So, why am i getting this error?
I have worked in an app that could access the destination locally by setting the full url in the .yml file, but it does not work when i do the same in my app.
Could anyone help me get through this problem?

entry.title: AI in SAP Build - Overview and Best Practices
entry.content:
AI in SAP Build: Transforming Business Applications

Introduction

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing the way businesses operate, and SAP Build is at the forefront of this transformation. SAP Build integrates AI capabilities to enhance application development, process automation, and overall business efficiency. This blog post provides an overview of AI in SAP Build and shares best practices in prompt engineering to help you maximize the potential of AI tools.

AI in SAP Build

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing the way businesses operate, and SAP Build is at the forefront of this transformation. SAP Build integrates AI capabilities to enhance application development, process automation, and overall business efficiency. This blog post provides an overview of AI in SAP Build and shares best practices in prompt engineering to help you maximize the potential of AI tools. 

What is SAP Build? 

SAP Build provides you the flexibility to create and extend apps, automate processes and design digital workspaces with low-code, pro-code and generative AI tools. Its collaborative design allows diverse teams to build solutions faster using prebuilt solutions, AI assistance, and seamless integration with SAP applications. 

General Topic: Best Practices in Prompt Engineering 

Before we start with all the AI features in detail, we have to cover a general topic. Prompt engineering is the art of crafting effective prompts to guide AI models in generating accurate and relevant outputs. For example prompts should be clear, specific, and directive. In this blog you can find all the details about the topic: Prompt Engineering

AI in SAP Build 

Coming to AI in SAP Build. SAP Build is a comprehensive suite designed to simplify the creation and customization of business applications. By incorporating AI, SAP Build empowers users to develop intelligent applications with minimal coding. This is a collection auf all the AI Capabilities in SAP Build. 

SAP Build Code 

  • Overview of all GenAI Options in SAP Build Code

 SAP Build Apps 

  • SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core  

SAP Build Process Automation 

  • Boost Development with GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation  
  • GenAI boosting SAP Build Process Automation  

SAP Build - ABAP Environments

  • ABAP AI  

Joule Studio 

  • TechEd Session about Joule Studio 

 

Conclusion 

AI in SAP Build is transforming the way businesses develop and customize applications, making it easier to integrate intelligent features and automate processes. By following best practices in prompt engineering, you can harness the full potential of AI tools, ensuring they deliver high-quality and relevant outputs. Embrace these strategies to enhance your AI interactions and drive innovation in your business. 

This blog will be constantly updated. 

Share your experience using the AI capabilities in SAP Build in the comments!

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hi @ClaudiaMayer -

Regarding @jalpanthakkar34's question about reporting CPP2, is SAP planning on addressing this issue?

It is also a problem for reporting QPP2 on the RL1. Further, the RL1 only provides one cumulation wage type for reporting both QPP and QPP2 employer portion, however, the RL1 summary info indicates that the two amounts should be reported separately. It looks like a new cumulation wage type is required.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025
entry.content:
Já temos data para mais uma edição do SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025, será em 26 de Abril de 2025. Um evento que reúne a Comunidade SAP para compartilhar conhecimento, fortalecer conexões profissionais e explorar as mais recentes inovações no ecossistema SAP.

Veja como foi a última edição: #SITSAL
A agenda do evento 2025 ainda está em desenvolvimento (A ser liberada em Março)

Um Pouco da Nossa História

O SAP Inside Track teve sua primeira edição no país em 2009, consolidando-se como um marco importante para a Comunidade SAP. Este evento faz parte de uma série global presente em diversos países, organizada por membros ativos da Comunidade SAP com o objetivo de promover o compartilhamento de conhecimento e estreitar laços profissionais.

No Brasil, o SAP Inside Track já conta com mais de 15 anos de existência, tendo passado por 11 cidades diferentes em mais de 40 edições. Cidades como São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, São Leopoldo, Joinville, Campinas, Salvador, Ribeirão Preto, Belo Horizonte, Curitiba, Fortaleza e Recife já receberam este importante evento.

O Que Esperar do Evento

O SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025 é a maior oportunidade de aprender, trocar experiências e conhecer outros profissionais do mercado SAP. Com palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócios, serão abordadas as mais diversas soluções SAP, incluindo as inovações do SAP S/4HANA e outras tecnologias emergentes.

Além disso, o evento é uma excelente chance de expandir seu networking, conectando-se pessoalmente com grandes nomes da Comunidade SAP e profissionais ativos no mercado.

Data: 26/04/2025
Local: A definir
Inscrição: Ainda não disponível (será realizada via Eventbrite)

Chamada para Palestrantes

Se você tem conhecimento para compartilhar, esta é sua oportunidade!
Envie sua proposta com o tema, título e uma breve sinopse para: sapinsidetrackbrazil@gmail.com

  • Duração máxima da palestra: 40 minutos
  • Prazo final para envio de propostas: Final de Fevereiro de 2025

Aguarde o contato da nossa equipe para confirmação.

Organizadores e Patrocínio

O SAP Inside Track Salvador é organizado por membros dedicados da Comunidade SAP, com o apoio de empresas e patrocinadores que viabilizam a realização do evento. Gostaríamos de agradecer a todos que contribuem para o sucesso deste encontro.

Apoio: Moovi Education

Junte-se a nós no SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025!

Esperamos você para um dia repleto de aprendizado, networking e inovação. Fique atento às atualizações e não perca esta oportunidade única!

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the SAP Enterprise Architecture Community Group!
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

I have been wotking as SAP SD consultant for a long time, I lke so much, but I am falling in love for how the conection between the IT and Business can do great things if them work closer. 

Because that, I am looking for a speacialist area when I can develope this hability, while my research I found the EA. I done a SAP Training EA foundantion but I want to be a specialist in this área.

Best regards

entry.title: Optimization on SELECT Statement using SORTED INTERNAL Table
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have used the below query using SORTED table, still the system consumes more time for execution.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2502 Release: Everything You Need to Know
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2502 release is around the corner! As part of SAP’s commitment to delivering two major upgrades per year, it’s essential to take the necessary steps to ensure a smooth transition. Since upgrade dates are fixed and apply to all customers simultaneously, being prepared is the key.

Key Milestones:

  • The Q-System will be upgraded first.

  • Two weeks later, the P-System will follow.

  • Regression testing occurs during the interim two-week period.

 

I have been supporting S/4HANA Public Cloud customers since 2017 as a Customer Success Manager and here are the top 5 steps to keep in mind to make this process seamless:

 

1. Review the Release Notes

Stay informed about the latest features, enhancements, and changes in the S/4HANA Public Cloud 2502 release by reviewing the release notes.

  • Use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Preliminary What's New Viewer (Public) to explore new and updated features.

  • Assess the impact of changes on your current scope using the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (Public). This is a powerful tool to take advantage of! You can see all the changes that impact your specific scope.

  • To activate a new feature, follow the guidelines in the Activate New Features (Public) app.

 

2. Check Business Roles in Your Q-System

Some Business Catalogs will be deprecated and replaced with new ones or renamed. Deprecated catalogs will be removed two releases later. To stay ahead:

  • Regularly review retired Business Catalogs and any new scope items.

  • Make sure to favorite the SAP Note 3533745 to stay up to date with changes. There is an excel file attached to the note which explains the changes.

  • Implement necessary changes promptly to ensure continuity.

 

3. Test Your Existing Business Processes

Conduct regression tests in your Q-System immediately after the upgrade. For efficient testing:

  • Utilize the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud to automate test scripts of your end-to-end business scenarios

  • If you opted for Post Upgrade Test (PUT), then SAP executes these smoke tests based on the active scope you have on your behalf as a means of helping you identify as early as possible where there may be regression issues to resolve. This is an optional free service that you can sign up

  • Refer to Testing in S/4HANA Cloud for more information.

  • Manually test the following:

    • Output management/forms

    • Business role/authorizations

    • Analytics

    • Interfaces

    • Extensions

    • Scenarios utilizing expert configuration are not covered by standard automated test scripts. Depending on the business process, your own automated test scripts can be created.

 

4. Create Regression Tickets

If defects are identified during regression testing, report them promptly via the SAP for Me platform.

  • Include ‘[R]’ in the Subject to indicate issues related to Upgrade Regression Testing.

  • These tickets are prioritized, ensuring faster resolution and code fixes.

 

5. Move the change project

During the two-week period between the Q and P upgrades, your Q-System and P-System will have different code bases, halting transport changes.

  • It is recommended to move existing business change projects (BCP) to P using the Q2P transport process latest the Wednesday prior to the Q-System upgrade.

  • If you plan to extend the scope ( activate a new scope item or country), make sure you plan testing and activation accordingly.

 

Register for 2502 Early Release Webinars: Want to explore the new features more deeply? Register for the webinars tailored to each Line of Business (LOB).

Is there any other steps that you would add? Let me know in the comments or send me a message!

entry.title: How to Select All Rows and Execute Custom Actions on a List Report-like Screen
entry.content:
Introduction

In the previous blog, we explored how to call RAP actions with table types from a List Report. While this approach works effectively for scenarios involving a few selected rows, the "Select All" functionality only selects the rows currently visible on the screen. This constraint makes it unsuitable for use cases requiring bulk selection of large datasets.

To address this limitation, this blog focuses on how to implement "Select All"-like functionality on a List Report-like screen to execute custom actions. By leveraging the Flexible Programming Model and creating a Custom Page, we can achieve bulk selection and execute actions on all rows, whether visible or not.

 

Implementation Approach

To achieve "Select All"-like functionality and execute actions on all rows, the following approach is used:

  • Retrieve Filter Conditions: Instead of selected rows, the filter conditions from the filter bar are passed to the action, allowing it to operate on all matching rows, even if they are not visible.
  • Customize the Filter Bar: The filter bar displays only fields relevant to the action, and the "Adapt Filter" button is hidden to prevent users from adding additional fields to the filter bar.

This application is not intended to be a generic List Report but is specifically for scenarios requiring bulk actions on all rows.

Note: This approach was implemented as part of a proof of concept (PoC) and has not been used in any productive applications.

 

Scenario Overview

In this blog, we will create an application similar to the one in the previous example, focusing on calculating stock values. The application allows users to specify a Plant (single value) and Location (multiple values) in the filter bar. When the "Get Total Stock Value" action is executed, the total stock value is calculated and displayed in a popup dialog.

 

Development Environment

  • Backend: BTP ABAP Environment (trial)
  • Frontend: SAP Business Application Studio

 

Development Steps

  • Backend

    • Table Definition
    • Defining an Action to Handle Deep Parameters
    • Implementing Behavior Logic
  • Frontend
    • Creating a Custom Page Application
    • Adjusting Filter Fields
    • Implementing the View
    • Implementing the Controller

 

Backend

The backend implementation for this application is similar to the one described in the previous blog. As such, the explanation has been omitted here, and only the code is provided for reference.

1. Table Definition

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Product Stock'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zyasu_stock {

  key client      : abap.clnt not null;
  key id          : sysuuid_x16 not null;
  product_id      : abap.char(20);
  plant           : abap.char(4);
  location        : abap.char(4);
  price           : abap.int2;
  stock           : abap.int2;
  created_by      : abp_creation_user;
  created_at      : abp_creation_tstmpl;
  last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user;
  last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;

}

 

 

2. Defining an Action to Handle Deep Parameters

2.1. Behavior Definition

 

static action calcStockAmountAll deep parameter ZA_PLANT result [1] ZA_AMOUNT;

 

2.2. Root Abstract Entity

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Plant'
define root abstract entity ZA_PLANT
{
    plant : abap.char(4);
    _location : composition [0..*] of ZA_LOCATION; 
}

 

2.3. Child Abstract Entity

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Location'
define abstract entity ZA_LOCATION
{
    location : abap.char(4);
    _plant : association to parent ZA_PLANT;
    
}

 

2.4. Abstract Behavior Definition

 

abstract;
strict ( 2 );
with hierarchy;

define behavior for ZA_PLANT alias Plant
{
  association _location;
}

define behavior for ZA_LOCATION alias Location
{
}

 

 

3. Implement Behavior Logic

 

  METHOD calcStockAmountAll.
    DATA plant TYPE zr_yasu_stock-Plant.
    DATA r_location TYPE RANGE OF zr_yasu_stock-location.
    DATA amount TYPE i.

    "get plant and storage locations
    LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(key).
      plant = key-%param-plant.
      r_location = VALUE #( FOR loc IN key-%param-_location (
                           sign = 'I'
                           option = 'EQ'
                           low = loc-location ) ).
      EXIT. "get the first record of the keys
    ENDLOOP.

    "select from cds view (not including draft data)
    SELECT price,
           stock
      FROM zr_yasu_stock
      WHERE plant = 
      AND location IN @r_location
      INTO TABLE (stock_t).

    " calculate stock amount
    LOOP AT stock_t INTO DATA(stock).
      amount = amount + stock-Price * stock-Stock.
    ENDLOOP.

    " return result
    result = VALUE #( FOR key1 IN keys (
                       %cid = key1-%cid
                       %param =  VALUE #( amount = amount )
                     ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

Frontend

1. Creating a Custom Page Application

In SAP Business Application Studio, select the OData service created in the previous step and use the "Custom Page" template to create the application.

Since we want to implement the logic using TypeScript, select the "Enable TypeScript" option in the "Project Attributes" during the project setup process.

The structure of the generated project is as follows:

2. Adjusting Filter Fields

Set the filter fields in the Page Map so that only Plant and Location are displayed in the filter bar.

3. Implementing the View

Implement the ext/main/Main.view.xml file as follows. Similar to the List Report, it displays a filter bar, a table, and variant management.

 

<mvc:View xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:macros="sap.fe.macros"
    xmlns:f="sap.f" xmlns:macrosTable="sap.fe.macros.table" xmlns:v="sap.ui.fl.variants"
    xmlns:html="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" controllerName="miyasuta.selectallaction.ext.main.Main">
    <f:DynamicPage id="FilterBarDefault" class="sapUiResponsiveContentPadding">
		<f:title>
			<f:DynamicPageTitle id="_IDGenDynamicPageTitle1">
				<f:heading>
					<v:VariantManagement id="vm" for="FilterBar" showSetAsDefault="true" headerLevel="H2" />
				</f:heading>
				<f:snappedContent>
					<Text id="_IDGenText" text="{fbConditions>/filtersTextInfo}" />
				</f:snappedContent>
			</f:DynamicPageTitle>
		</f:title>
		<f:header>
			<f:DynamicPageHeader id="_IDGenDynamicPageHeader1" pinnable="true">
				<VBox id="_IDGenVBox1">
					<macros:FilterBar
						metaPath="/Stock/@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionFields"
						id="FilterBar"
						filterChanged=".onFiltersChanged"
					/>
				</VBox>
			</f:DynamicPageHeader>
		</f:header>
        <f:content>
			<macros:Table metaPath="@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem" readOnly="true" id="LineItemTable" filterBar="FilterBar">
                <macros:actions>
                    <macrosTable:Action id="_IDGenAction1"
                        key="getTotalStockValue"
                        text="Get Total Stock Value"
                        press=".onGetTotalStockValue"
                        requiresSelection="false"
                    />
                </macros:actions>
            </macros:Table>
		</f:content>
    </f:DynamicPage>
</mvc:View>

 

 

4. Implementing the Controller

The complete code is available here.

4.1. Hiding the "Adapt Filter" Button

To hide the "Adapt Filter" button, set the visible property of the button to false in the onBeforeRendring method. While this approach may not be officially recommended, I found it in the following Q&A:

CAP: Disable visibility of Adapt Filters button in List Page

 

    public  onBeforeRendering(): void {        
        //disable the adapt filter button
        const adaptFilter = this.getView()?.byId("miyasuta.selectallaction::StockMain--FilterBar-content-btnAdapt") as Button;
		adaptFilter.setVisible(false);
    }

 

The button's ID can be identified using tools like the UI5 Inspector.

4.2. Calling the Action

First, retrieve the selected values from the filter bar. If the required fields are not specified, return an error.
Next, set the retrieved values as parameters and call the action. Finally, display the result in a popup dialog.

    public onGetTotalStockValue(): void {
        //get filter conditions
        const { plant, locations } = this.extractFilterConditions();

        // validate required parameters
        if (!plant) {
            MessageBox.error("Select a plant in the filter");
            return;
        }

        // invoke action
        const model = this.getModel();
        const operation = model?.bindContext("/Stock/" + namespace + "calcStockAmountAll(...)") as ODataContextBinding;
        operation.setParameter("plant", plant);
        operation.setParameter("_location", locations);

        const fnSuccess = () => {
            // show total
            const result = operation.getBoundContext().getObject() as Result;
            const amount = result.amount;
            const numberFormat = NumberFormat.getIntegerInstance({
                groupingEnabled: true
            });
            const formattedAmount = numberFormat.format(amount);
            MessageBox.show(`${formattedAmount} JPY`, {
                title: "Stock Amount"
            });
        };

        const fnError = (error: Error) => {
            MessageBox.error(error.message);
        };

        operation.invoke().then(fnSuccess, fnError);
    }

This is the part of the code that extracts filter conditions from the filter bar, processes them, and returns the extracted plant and location(s).

    private extractFilterConditions(): { plant: string | undefined; locations: Location[] } {
        //get filter conditions
        const allFilters = this.getAllFilters();

        let plantRef = { plant: undefined as string | undefined };
        let locations: Array<Location> = [];

        allFilters.forEach(filter => {
            if (filter.getPath()) {
                this.processFilter(filter, plantRef, locations);
            } else {
                filter.getFilters()?.forEach(innerFilter => this.processFilter(innerFilter, plantRef, locations));
            }
        });

        return { plant: plantRef.plant, locations };
    }

    private getAllFilters(): Filter[] {
        const filterBar = this.getView()?.byId("FilterBar") as FilterBar;

        // Get the filters from the FilterBar; the structure of filters[0]
        // can either be an object (if a single filter value is set)
        // or an array (if multiple filter values are set).
        const filters = (filterBar.getFilters() as FilterObject).filters[0];

        // If filters[0] contains an array of filters, return it directly
        if (Array.isArray(filters?.getFilters())) {
            return filters.getFilters() as Filter[];

        // If filters[0] is an object, wrap it in an array and return
        } else if (filters) {
            return [filters];
        }
        // If no filters are set, return an empty array
        return [];
    }
    
    private processFilter(filter: Filter, plantRef: { plant: string | undefined }, locations: Location[]): void {
        if (filter.getPath() === "Plant") {
            plantRef.plant = filter.getValue1();
        } else if (filter.getPath() === "Location") {
            locations.push({ location: filter.getValue1() });
        }
    }

 

Conclusion

This blog addressed the challenge of being unable to select all rows and execute an action in a List Report. To overcome this, values specified in the filter bar were passed as parameters to the action, achieving functionality similar to "Select All."

While I considered implementing a similar approach by extending the List Report, I found it to be less feasible for two reasons: the difficulty of retrieving values from the filter bar, and the fact that using the internal controls of the filter bar itself is not appropriate. This made the approach an unsuitable solution.

The Flexible Programming Model is well-suited for creating applications with a List Report-like appearance while enabling more flexible extensions.

If you have solved a similar challenge using a different approach, I would appreciate it if you could share your solution in the comments section.

entry.title: Re: Contract-Based Revenue Recognition
entry.content:
Thank you for your set of questions, @joanabezerra ,

let me get a stab at the first two: According to the SAP Help documentation for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition,

  • "[P]rovider contracts are not created and changed in S/4HANA Cloud" 1.  
  • "You use an API to create provider contracts in the S/4HANA Cloud system" 2.

That said, it looks like there is no other way to create provider contracts, using the SOAP APIs appears to be the only way.

entry.title: Re: How to call public cloud SAP API from embedded steampunk?
entry.content: Hi nicmon, API_SALES_ORDER is a remote API (see https://api.sap.com/api/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV). For local access, please use the I_SalesOrderTP Business Object Interface (see https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_SALESORDERTP). Best regards, Kai

Here is an OData API for the on-prem. version and there are equivalents for both the public cloud and private cloud options too. https://api.sap.com/api/OP_PRODUCT_0002/resource/Product_Plant

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Flexible Workflow Manager roles
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
we use the flexible workflow for supplier invoices. Blocked invoices should first be released by the purchase order creator and then by their manager (role: manager of the last approver). I created the corresponding workflow in the app “Manage workflows for supplier invoices”. I maintained the organizational structure in PPOME, marked the manager's position as “head of the organizational unit” and assigned the employees as people. The employees are also maintained as business partners and the communication data was maintained in the personnel master record (PA30). The manager can also be found in the table IEMPLOYMENTMGR.

The creator of the order is found. However, when the supplier releases the supplier invoice, the error message "A technical error occurred while resolving the processor rule "/RULE/MRM_M_L_APP" appears. The analysis states "Manager of the last released. could not be determined as agent."

Do you have any idea why this error occurs and why the manager cannot be found?

Thank you very much and best regards.

Essentially, I need to implement the 4 CRUD operations without persisting the model in the database, as it will consume another service. entry.title: Re: Public Cloud - Consume local service/API in ABAP code
entry.content:
Thank you for your request, @nicmon ,

have you considered using a so-called partner function in the Header Partner segment of the API? It does allow for Personnel (8 characters) or Contact Person ID (10 char). While you may not be able to change the 'createdBy' field for sales orders created via API, this way, you might be able to select a different field to determine the actual 'owner' of each sales order.    

If this helped you solve your request, please mark this response accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: BAPI for MB52 and MMSC
entry.content:
Are you on a recent SAP-System (S/4)? If so, you should look into CDS / APIs like "API_MATERIAL_STOCK_SRV" instead of BAPIs ...
For instance, you could check the OData-service "MMIM_MULTIPLE_MATERIAL_SRV" behind the "F1595"-App, which is similar to "MB52". I don't think there are released objects like BAPIs to match "MB52".

A BAPI for "MMSC" would be "BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA".
Another Approach would be to use "LSMW" or Trx. "MMSC_MASS".

Hope this helps

Nic T.

entry.title: Re: Calling BAPIs in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Here is the answer that I've got from SAP Support:

"Note 2447593 has moved to the sap help portal and is linked from the API Hub: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/2cf48091d5864284ac4541b86a8737fd.html

Both APIs that you listed (BAPI_INQUIRY_CREATEFROMDATA2 and BAPI_QUOTATION_CREATEFROMDATA2) are not mentioned in this document, thus you cannot use them in S/4HANA Public Cloud. For requesting any kind of API we offer the corresponding customer influence channel at SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Extensibility and Integration (Remote APIs), see also https://api.sap.com/products/SAPS4HANACloud/overview. This also replaces note 2653671."

entry.title: Re: problems with MKD client
entry.content: Check your BrandedSettings.json.  I suspect the values for ConnectionSettings are incorrect or left at the default template values.  There may be other issues with BrandedSettings as well so please check the whole file.

entry.title: Re: SAP SSAM2210 app update failed issue
entry.content:
When upgrading it is always recommended to check the SAP Service and Asset Manager - MDK Support Matrix in case there are SAP Notes needing to be applied related to metadata changes. In this case the version you are using is now out of maintenance and not listed as a supported combination.

You may want to use an older MDK / Extension combination that is supported instead of the Play store generic client. You also may want to contact support to see what options you have.

entry.title: Re: SAP SSAM2210 app update failed issue
entry.content:
When upgrading it is always recommended to check the SAP Service and Asset Manager - MDK Support Matrix in case there are SAP Notes needing to be applied related to metadata changes. In this case, the version you are using is listed as out of maintenance and not listed as a supported combination.

You may want to use an older MDK / Extension combination that is supported instead of the Play store generic client. You also may want to contact support to see what options you have.

entry.title: Account Code and Name Query with Parent and Child in same Row
entry.content:
Hello Experts

I found an awesome query which I managed to modify to suit, but need it modified a bit more and I havent been able to resolve it, a bit out of my capacity and i am not sure if it can be achieved

So, what I need is the results to show the Parent part number and names to be repeated so that the parent code and name and in the same rows as the child code and names
I have attached two txt files one with the Actual results I get when I run the query, and one txt file which what is my expectation of the results when I run the query (for privacy reasons I have cleared the data from names columns)
Below is the query (i tried to upload the files in Excel format, but unable to do so, hence had to save as txt)

 

SELECT 
    TBL1.[GROUP],
    TBL1.[Main Account] AS [Main Account],
    TBL1.[Main Account Name] AS [Main Account Name],
    TBL1.[Sub Account] AS [Sub Account],
    TBL1.[Sub Account Name] AS [Sub Account Name],
    TBL1.[Detailed Account] AS [Detailed Account],
    TBL1.[Detailed Account Name] AS [Detailed Account Name],
    TBL1.[Segmented Account] AS [Segmented Account],
    TBL1.[Segmented Account Name] AS [Segmented Account Name],
    TBL1.[Sub-Segmented Account] AS [Sub-Segmented Account],
    TBL1.[Sub-Segmented Account Name] AS [Sub-Segmented Account Name]
FROM (
    SELECT 
        t0.groupmask, 
        t0.grpline, 
        t0.levels, 
        T0.[AcctCode] AS [Main Account], 
        T0.[AcctName] AS [Main Account Name],
        '-' AS [Sub Account], 
        '-' AS [Sub Account Name],
        '-' AS [Detailed Account],
        '-' AS [Detailed Account Name],
        T0.[Segment_0] + '-' + T0.[Segment_1] + '-' + T0.[Segment_2] AS [Segmented Account],
        '-' AS [Segmented Account Name],
        '-' AS [Sub-Segmented Account],
        '-' AS [Sub-Segmented Account Name],
        (CASE T0.[GroupMask] 
            WHEN 1 THEN 'Asset' 
            WHEN 2 THEN 'Liabilities' 
            WHEN 3 THEN 'Equity' 
            WHEN 4 THEN 'Revenue' 
            WHEN 5 THEN 'COGS' 
            WHEN 6 THEN 'Expenses' 
            WHEN 7 THEN 'Other Income & Expense' 
        END) AS [GROUP]
    FROM OACT T0 
    WHERE t0.Levels = '1'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        t0.groupmask,
        t0.grpline,
        t0.levels,
        '-',
        '-',
        T0.[AcctCode] AS [Sub Account], 
        T0.[AcctName] AS [Sub Account Name],
        '-' AS [Detailed Account],
        '-' AS [Detailed Account Name],
        '-' AS [Segmented Account],
        '-' AS [Segmented Account Name],
        '-' AS [Sub-Segmented Account],
        '-' AS [Sub-Segmented Account Name],
        (CASE T0.[GroupMask] 
            WHEN 1 THEN 'Asset' 
            WHEN 2 THEN 'Liabilities' 
            WHEN 3 THEN 'Equity' 
            WHEN 4 THEN 'Revenue' 
            WHEN 5 THEN 'COGS' 
            WHEN 6 THEN 'Expenses' 
            WHEN 7 THEN 'Other Income & Expense' 
        END) AS [GROUP]
    FROM OACT T0 
    WHERE t0.Levels = '2'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        t0.groupmask,
        t0.grpline,
        t0.levels,
        '-',
        '-',
        '-' AS [Sub Account],
        '-' AS [Sub Account Name],
        T0.[AcctCode] AS [Detailed Account], 
        T0.[AcctName] AS [Detailed Account Name],
        '-' AS [Segmented Account],
        '-' AS [Segmented Account Name],
        '-' AS [Sub-Segmented Account],
        '-' AS [Sub-Segmented Account Name],
        (CASE T0.[GroupMask] 
            WHEN 1 THEN 'Asset' 
            WHEN 2 THEN 'Liabilities' 
            WHEN 3 THEN 'Equity' 
            WHEN 4 THEN 'Revenue' 
            WHEN 5 THEN 'COGS' 
            WHEN 6 THEN 'Expenses' 
            WHEN 7 THEN 'Other Income & Expense' 
        END) AS [GROUP]
    FROM OACT T0 
    WHERE t0.Levels = '3'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        t0.groupmask,
        t0.grpline,
        t0.levels,
        '-',
        '-',
        '-' AS [Sub Account],
        '-' AS [Sub Account Name],
        '-' AS [Detailed Account],
        '-' AS [Detailed Account Name],
        T0.[Segment_0] + '-' + T0.[Segment_1] + '-' + T0.[Segment_2] AS [Segmented Account],
        T0.[AcctName] AS [Segmented Account Name],
        '-' AS [Sub-Segmented Account],
        '-' AS [Sub-Segmented Account Name],
        (CASE T0.[GroupMask] 
            WHEN 1 THEN 'Asset' 
            WHEN 2 THEN 'Liabilities' 
            WHEN 3 THEN 'Equity' 
            WHEN 4 THEN 'Revenue' 
            WHEN 5 THEN 'COGS' 
            WHEN 6 THEN 'Expenses' 
            WHEN 7 THEN 'Other Income & Expense' 
        END) AS [GROUP]
    FROM OACT T0 
    WHERE t0.Levels = '4'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        t0.groupmask,
        t0.grpline,
        t0.levels,
        '-',
        '-',
        '-' AS [Sub Account],
        '-' AS [Sub Account Name],
        '-' AS [Detailed Account],
        '-' AS [Detailed Account Name],
        '-' AS [Segmented Account],
        '-' AS [Segmented Account Name],
        T0.[Segment_0] + '-' + T0.[Segment_1] + '-' + T0.[Segment_2] AS [Sub-Segmented Account], 
        T0.[AcctName] AS [Sub-Segmented Account Name],
        (CASE T0.[GroupMask] 
            WHEN 1 THEN 'Asset' 
            WHEN 2 THEN 'Liabilities' 
            WHEN 3 THEN 'Equity' 
            WHEN 4 THEN 'Revenue' 
            WHEN 5 THEN 'COGS' 
            WHEN 6 THEN 'Expenses' 
            WHEN 7 THEN 'Other Income & Expense' 
        END) AS [GROUP]
    FROM OACT T0 
    WHERE t0.Levels = '5'
) TBL1
ORDER BY
    TBL1.groupmask,
    TBL1.grpline,
    TBL1.levels

 





Regards
Rah

entry.title: 【日本語化】Generative AI with SAP - Part3: Joule: SAPのGenerative AI Copilot
entry.content:
このブログはEcosystem Growth活動の一環で「Generative AI with SAP（@Raja_Gupta）」シリーズを日本語化・加筆したものです。このシリーズでは、SAPがポートフォリオ全体でどのように AI および Generative AI を活用しているかを理解することに焦点を当てています。

このシリーズで学べること：

  • SAPがポートフォリオ全体で AI および Generative AI をどのように活用しているか
  • SAP Business AI とは何か、そしてSAPのパートナーや顧客がそれをどのように活用できるのか
  • SAP AI Core・SAP AI Launchpad・Joule とは何か
  • SAPのAI戦略においてSAP BTPが果たす役割
  • 実際のユースケースやハンズオン事例など

理解しやすくするため、このブログシリーズは短いセクションに分けられています。それぞれのブログは、10～15分程度で学習できる内容です。

  • Part1 - SAP Business AIの概要
  • Part2 - SAP Business AIの構成要素
  • Part3 - Joule：SAPのGenerative AI Copilot [現在のブログ]
  • Part4 - SAP BTPにおけるAI Foundation 
  • Part5 - SAP AI Coreの概要
  • Part6 - SAP AI Launchpadの概要
  • Part7 - SAP AI CoreとSAP AI Launchpadの使い方
  • Part8 - SAP AI CoreにおけるHello World
  • Part9 - Generative AI Hubの概要
  • Part10 - SAP HANA Cloud vector engine
  • Part11 - RAG（Retrieval-Augmented Generation）・SAP AI Core・Generative AI Hub・HANA Cloud vector engine を用いた実際のユースケース

このブログはシリーズの第3回目であり、SAPのGenerative AI CopilotであるJouleについて学びます。

英語版の原著ブログはこちらからご覧いただけます。

Note: 後続のブログも順次公開予定です。

 

Jouleとは？

信頼できるAIのパートナーがあなたのそばにいて、自然な言語と生成能力を駆使し、SAPのクラウドポートフォリオ全体で日々の業務をサポート・向上させてくれる生成AI Copilot、それがJouleです。
以下にJouleの主要ポイントをまとめました。

それぞれのポイントについて詳しく見ていく前に、Jouleをよりよく理解するため、いくつかの重要な用語を簡単に整理していきましょう。

 

Generative AI (生成AI)とは？

生成AIは、芸術作品、音楽、リアルな画像など、新しいものを作り出すことができる人工知能（AI）の一種です。あらかじめ何を作るかを具体的に指示されなくても、新しいものを生み出せるという特徴があります。
生成AIは、既存のデータからパターンを学ぶことによって新しいコンテンツを作り出すことができます。まるでクリエイティブな機械が、見たデータを直接コピーすることなく、アートや音楽、テキストなど、新しいものを想像して生成するようなものです。
生成AIの基本を学ぶには、本ブログの基となる英語版著者が公開している「Generative AI for Beginners」をご覧ください。
AI、機械学習（ML）、生成AIについて非常にシンプルな形で理解したい場合は、同じく本ブログの英語版著者が公開している「How I Explained AI, Machine Learning and Generative AI to My 5 Year-old Kid」をご参照ください。

 

AI Copilotとは？

ちょうど副操縦士がパイロットを補佐して飛行機を操縦するように、AI Copilotは様々なタスクであなたを助けるスマートなアシスタントとして機能します。
AI Copilotは人工知能を活用してあなたのニーズを理解し、提案やサポートを提供するツールです。これにより、作業がより簡単で効率的になります。

AI Copilotはどのように動作するのでしょうか？

技術的には、AI Copilotは大規模言語モデル（LLM: Large Language Model）を使用して、ユーザーを様々なタスクや意思決定プロセスでサポートする会話型インターフェースです。
LLMを活用することで、AI Copilotは膨大な量のデータを理解・分析・処理する能力を持っています。
簡単な例を挙げます。例えば、あなたがPCでレポートを書いているとします。AI Copilotは、あなたが書いている内容を理解し、より良い言葉や表現を提案したり、スペルチェックや文法の修正を行ったり、レポートに含めるべき関連情報を見つける手助けをしてくれるツールとして機能します。
要するに、AI Copilotは複雑なタスクを簡素化し、貴重なガイダンスとサポートを提供することで、最終的にはユーザーの体験を向上させ、ビジネスが効果的かつ効率的に目標に向かうことを支援します。

市場におけるいくつかのAI Copilot

Jouleの他にも、以下のようなAI Copilotサービスが展開されています。

・Microsoft Copilot
MS Office製品と連携し、Word・Excel・PowerPointなどのアプリケーションにおけるサポートを提供

・GitHub Copilot
より効率的かつ効果的なコード開発を支援

Data Contextualization (データの文脈化)とは？

Data Contextualization (データの文脈化) とは、データに関連する情報を追加して、より実用的なものにするプロセスです。これには、背景情報やパターン、トレンド、アウトライヤー（外れ値）などを含め、データが何を意味しているのかユーザが理解しやすくするための手法が含まれます。
それではここから、本ブログのメインテーマであるJouleについて見ていきましょう！

 

改めて、Jouleとは？

信頼できるAIパートナーがあなたのそばにいて、自然言語の理解・生成能力を活用し、SAPのクラウドポートフォリオ全体で日々の業務を支援・向上させてくれる存在、それがJouleです。

Jouleは次のような特徴を持っています：

  • 先進的な生成AI Copilot
  • SAPのクラウドエンタープライズポートフォリオ全体にEmbeddedされた(埋め込まれた)存在
  • SAPのアプリケーションやサードパーティのソースから幅広く深い情報を取り込み、能動的かつ文脈に基づいたインサイトを提供

Jouleは、人々がより迅速に仕事を進め、安全かつコンプライアンスに準拠した方法でより良いビジネス成果を上げるのを支援するよう設計されています。

 

お客様のJoule活用イメージ

Jouleは複数のシステムからデータを迅速に整理し、文脈を追加してスマートなインサイトを提供します。
Jouleは、人事、財務、サプライチェーン、調達、顧客体験などの領域に関するSAPアプリケーション、およびSAP BTP: Business Technology Platformに組み込まれます。Jouleは、SAPのビジネスデータおよびビジネスプロセスで事前に学習が行われています。
ユーザーは単に質問をしたり、問題を平易な言葉で述べるだけで、SAPのポートフォリオおよびサードパーティソース全体から膨大なビジネスデータを基にしたインテリジェントな回答を、文脈を維持したまま受け取ることができます。

 

例えば、製造業者が販売実績をより良く理解するためにJouleにヘルプを求めるシチュエーションを想像してみてください。Jouleは販売が低迷している地域を特定し、サプライチェーンの問題を明らかにする他のデータセットとリンクし、その後、サプライチェーンシステムに自動的に接続して製造業者が検討できる潜在的な対策を提案することができます。
また、HR（人事）の分野では、Jouleは偏りのない求人情報を作成したり、適切な面接質問を生成するのにも役立ちます。

 

JouleはSAP Conversational AIの後継ですか？

明確に言うと、JouleはSAP Conversational AIの後継として位置づけられるわけではなく、むしろ進化形や補完的な存在と考えることができます。SAP Conversational AIは主にチャットボットや自然言語処理にフォーカスしたツールですが、Jouleはそれを含めて、さらに高度な生成AIやデータの文脈化機能を提供します。
Jouleは、SAPのクラウドエンタープライズポートフォリオ全体にわたるデータを基に、プロアクティブかつ文脈に基づいたインサイトを提供するため、より広範な業務プロセスを支援することが可能です。つまり、JouleはSAP Conversational AIの機能を超えて、ユーザーにとってさらに多くの価値を生み出す次世代のAI Copilotと言えるでしょう。

 

おさえておくべきJouleの重要事項

Jouleの重要事項は以下の3点です。

SAPアプリケーションとの統合

Jouleは、SAPアプリケーションに統合された会話型ユーザーインターフェースを提供します。これは、SAP Fiori準拠のUIコントロールを使用してアシスタントの応答を表示するリッチなウェブクライアントです。

エンタープライズ対応

Jouleは、SAPバックエンドシステムとの即時に統合され、利用可能となります。また、AI倫理、GDPR、プライバシーコントロールに準拠しており、SOC-IIのコンプライアンスも維持しています。

自動アップデート

Jouleは、機能が追加または変更されるたびに自動的にアップデートされます。

 

SAP SuccessFactors HXMにおけるJoule活用

SAP SuccessFactorsで既にJouleが利用可能です
SAP SuccessFactorsのユーザーは、現在SAPの新しい生成AIアシスタントJouleを使ってHRタスクを実行することができます。例えば、従業員は質問に対する回答を得たり、休暇申請の承認や却下、名前や勤務地、個人代名詞の変更などのHR関連タスクを完了できます。
詳細については、アナウンスメントおよびヘルプドキュメントを参照してください。
また、erik.ebert2によるブログ「Say hello to Joule – Your Digital Assistant for SuccessFactors and other SAP Cloud products」もご覧ください。

 

SAP Business Application StudioにおけるJoule活用

JouleはSAP Business Application Studioに組み込まれていますが、一部制限があります。
詳細については、xinyeによるブログ「Joule on SAP Business Application Studio」を参照してください。

 

Jouleに関する最新情報を取得するには？

Jouleに関する最新情報の取得に役立つサイトをご紹介します。

  • SAP Joule product page
  • SAP Community for Joule

 

次のトピック

[日本語化] Generative AI with SAP - Part4 - SAP BTPにおけるAI Foundation

entry.title: SAP CodeJam: Una Experiencia que Paraguay Necesita
entry.content:
¡Hola comunidad SAP Paraguay! Quiero compartir con ustedes una experiencia increíble que tuvimos en agosto del 2024 en Buenos Aires, Argentina. Tuve la oportunidad de participar en el SAP CodeJam, y créanme, fue una experiencia que nos hizo pensar: "¿Por qué no hacer algo así en Paraguay?"

¿Qué es el SAP CodeJam?

SAP CodeJam es un evento práctico de 5 a 6 horas donde los participantes comparten conocimientos y desarrollan colaborativamente con tecnologías, plataformas y herramientas SAP en un ambiente divertido y casual. Son eventos enfocados en la comunidad de desarrolladores y respaldados por SAP, donde se exploran las tecnologías disponibles a través del SAP Developer Center.

¿Lo mejor? Es un evento hands-on, lo que significa que no solo escuchas sobre la tecnología, ¡sino que la usas y experimentas directamente! En esta ocasión, el evento se enfocó en dos temas super interesantes:

• SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code
• Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh


Lo Más Destacado del Evento

Nuestros Guías, Tuvimos el privilegio de contar con dos Developer Advocates de primer nivel, Antonio Maradiaga y Josh Bentley. Lo mejor de todo: ¡Antonio habla español fluido! Esto hizo que la barrera del idioma no fuera un problema para nadie.

Lo Que Aprendimos

En la sesión de SAP Build, nos sumergimos en:

• Creación de aplicaciones para tiendas online
• Automatización de procesos de negocio
• Desarrollo de interfaces sin escribir código
• Conexión con sistemas SAP S/4HANA
• Implementación de capacidades móviles

En la parte de Event-driven integrations, trabajamos en:

• Escenarios prácticos de integración
• Configuración de SAP Integration Suite

¿Por Qué Paraguay Necesita Esto?

Mientras participaba en el evento, no podía dejar de pensar en el potencial que tendría algo así en Paraguay. Nuestra comunidad SAP está creciendo, y eventos como este podrían:

• Fortalecer la red de profesionales SAP en Paraguay
• Crear oportunidades de networking directo con expertos
• Impulsar la innovación en nuestras empresas

 

Conclusión

El SAP CodeJam de Buenos Aires nos mostró el poder de reunir a la comunidad SAP para aprender y crecer juntos. Es hora de que Paraguay tome la iniciativa y organice eventos similares. ¿Están listos para ser parte de este cambio?
¡Comparte tus ideas en los comentarios! Juntos podemos hacer crecer la comunidad SAP en Paraguay.


¡Hagámoslo Realidad!

Me gustaría conocer tu opinión, comenta aquí, Qué temas les gustaría ver en un CodeJam en Paraguay? Te interesa SAP Build, BTP, ABAP Cloud, UI5, Fiori

entry.title: Re: Create a formula in a query for a text characteristic
entry.content: If you are using standard objects in SAP BW, then I am surprised you would need to do this. 0COUNTRY is an SAP delivered object configured as CHAR(3). The ID in this case would be 'BR' as per your example and the text as 'Brazil'. The Keys and Texts are loaded from standard datasource 0COUNTRY_TEXT. Perhaps the requirement is the 'Null' bit if the Country is not Brazil?

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Monte Carlo Simulation
entry.content:
Hi ChenNee,

Thank you for this feature and a short, but very informative blog post about Monte Carlo technique as such, and how it will look like in SAC at high level. 

I'm genuinely excited about the interface, pre-delivered sampling, and that it can run on top of existing models.

A couple of questions I would like to ask:

1. You mentioned that formula or definition of relation between the impacted KPI and the drivers is required, which totally makes sense. The question, is it relying on Account dimension and/or Measure formulas or Data Actions (Advanced Formulas) or can do both?

2. You mentioned that we can build Compass simulation on top of Time Series predictive forecast results. However in the case of Time Series model, there are no formulas and definitions as this is a 'black box' function. Is my understanding correct that Drivers that are predicted using Time Series model can't be used as Drivers for Compass simulation and will be kept static while other Drivers can be modelled and sampled?

Thank you

Kind regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Obsolete BW 0FISPCER time data - How to kill that right ?
entry.content: I would suggest checking config for Fiscal Year settings and maintenance. In SPRO look for 'Maintain fiscal year variant'.

entry.title: Parsing out XML field to create new dimensions
entry.content:
I have a table with 7 fields:  

SELECT [Policy_ID]
,[DateAdded]
,[DateUpdated]
,[PartyRecords]
,[AccountNumber]
,[UpdatedBy]
,[UpdatedOn]
FROM [Policy_Stat]

The PartyRecords field stores XML that looks like this:

In my universe, using IDT, I need to pull each party record out into it's own set of dimensions - PartyTypeCode, PartyName, PartyCorrespondenceName, LocationAddressLine1, LocationAddressLine2, LocationCity, LocationStateCode, LocationPostalCode, PartyRole. 

In the case above, I would end up with 3 sets of dimensions.  In sql, I would use cross apply, for example:

SELECT PartyRecord.value('PartyName[1]', 'nvarchar(100)') AS PartyName

FROM Policy_Stat

CROSS APPLY PartyRecords.nodes('/ArrayOfPartyRecord/PartyRecord') AS X(PartyRecord)

However, I'm not sure how to approach this in IDT.  Any suggestions would be welcome.

entry.title: AM049 error during employee and business partner synchronization
entry.content:
1. How to trigger the synchronization from Employee to BP

To trigger that, we can run transaction code SE38 and enter report /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE, input employee number, and execute.

2. Why this issue happens?

During the synchronization, if the employee has an user assigned on infotype 0105 subtype 01, the report will convert the IDADTYPE of the user from 00 to 04. It is necessary to delete and create the user again to do it. However, if the user has any entry in some tables, like ADRVP, the user cannot be deleted and, therefore, the above error message is generated.

Usually only 2 entries are expect of an SU01-User. These 2 correct entries are (at APPL_TABLE):

  • USR21
  • SOUD

Concrete information can be found in 
KBA 2578294 - FAQ - Employee to Business Partner synchronization in S/4HANA OnPremise
KBA 3094750 - Business User Management - FAQ 

3. How to check ADRVP

Let us review table PA0105 to find the user id linked to the employee firstly.


And then, review table USR21 to find the person number of the user.

See KBA 3234879 - AM049 error during employee and business partner synchronization

4. how to resolve this issue

If those entries originate from BCS (tables BCST_CAM, SODM, SOER, SOMG or SOSU) report ZRSBCS_RELEASE_BC01 from SAP Note 3228710 can be used to convert the usages.

If the error still persist after the report ZRSBCS_RELEASE_BC01 was executed then also execute the steps from the resolution part of the SAP KBA 2264162 - Person could not be deleted as it is still used - AM049

Hope this blog can help you.

Best Regards,
Grace

entry.title: Re: An example of AMDP( ABAP Managed Database Procedure ) in 740
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" HANA store procedure, with the help of AMDP class CL_DEMO_AMDP.

An AMDP class to handle with HANA stuff must inherit the tag interface IF_AMDP_MARKER_HDB. "

 

🙂

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: ABAP News for 7.40, SP08 - ABAP Managed Database Procedures (AMDP)
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" If your ABAP programs should run on HANA  only (or if you want to offer special implementations for HANA and alternative implementations for other databases), database procedures written in SQLScript offer a convenient access to the capabilites of HANA. In order to facilitate this Native access to HANA, in 7.40, SP05 the concept of ABAP Managed Database Procedures (AMDP) was introduced, that allows you to code SQLScript in special methods of normal ABAP classes and the runtime environment does the rest for you. (But please note that a single SQL statement does not become faster if you code it in an AMDP, how should it? As long as you can stay open, stay open. There is no drawback in performance if you can express the logics in single statements or in CDS views.  "

 

Such a nice sharing, thank you so much.

 

🙂

 

 

Regards,

ZFiori.
